publication.theme: SL Green and Mori Building Double Down on Office Development at 346 Madison

slides.headline: SL Green and Mori Building Double Down on Office Development at 346 Madison
slides.subhead: On a Tuesday in late May, Marc Holliday sat across from a familiar partner and signed away 49 percent of a development site. The buyer: Mori Building, the Japanese developer that...

slides.headline: The clean read
bullets: The buyer: Mori Building, the Japanese developer that has co-invested with SL Green on One...
The deal values the 850,000-square-foot project at $175 million.
On a Tuesday in late May, Marc Holliday sat across from a familiar partner and signed away...

slides.headline: The numbers tell the story
bullets: SL Green bought the site for $160 million last year.
That $15 million markup over twelve months is not a flip.

slides.headline: This is a capital stack story
slides.subhead: One Vanderbilt leased up rapidly in a market where most office towers sat empty.
bullets: The discount reflects the market's skepticism toward office development generally.
The premium reflects the specific attributes of this site and sponsor.
slides.quote: It is a signal: institutional capital sees a path to rent growth in the highest-quality office product, even as the broader market struggles with vacancy and repricing.

slides.headline: Who is moving the market
bullets: Marc Holliday: central to the transaction.
SL Green: central to the transaction.
One Vanderbilt: central to the transaction.

slides.headline: Where the deal can break
bullets: It is a signal: institutional capital sees a path to rent growth in the highest-quality office...
Mori Building is not a passive capital source.
It bought an 11 percent stake in One Vanderbilt in 2024 at a $4.7 billion valuation.

slides.headline: What lenders and investors notice
bullets: SL Green bought the site for $160 million last year.
That $15 million markup over twelve months is not a flip.

slides.headline: The broader implication
slides.subhead: The discount reflects the market's skepticism toward office development generally.
bullets: The premium reflects the specific attributes of this site and sponsor.
Holliday's statement that now is "the perfect time" to develop is not marketing fluff.

slides.headline: By then, the supply of new trophy office will be measured in single digits of buildings.
slides.subhead: The headline is the transaction. The story is the structure.
slides.kicker: If the capital stack works, the deal becomes a signal. If it does not, it becomes a warning.
